rulings.Case Title: VALLEY INTERNATIONAL ACADEMY VS. AMERICAN L & L EDUCATION, INC., A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Responses To Special Interrogatories And For Costs And Expenses, Including Attorneys' Fees Against The Plaintiff
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, July 11, 2014, Line 7, PLAINTIFF VALLEY INTERNATIONAL ACADEMY'S Motion For Order Compelling Responses To Special Interrogatories And For Costs And Expenses, Including Attorneys' Fees Against The Plaintiff. Granted. No opposition filed. The request to continue is denied. Within 10 days of the hearing date, Defendant American L&L Education shall serve verified responses to Plaintiff's Special Interrogatories, Set Two, without objection. All objections are waived; Defendant failed to timely serve its initial responses. Defendant's argument that the parties had agreed to service by email is unavailing; there is no evidence that the parties agreed to service by email to the exclusion of other permissible forms of service. Monetary sanctions of $ 1,660.00 are awarded in favor of Plaintiff Valley International Academy and against Defendant American L&L Education, Inc. to be paid within 30 days of service of notice of entry of order. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: THE CROSSINGS ASSOCIATES VS. STUDIO HOMES # 1 et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, July 11, 2014, Line 12, CROSS DEFENDANT FIRST AMERICAN TITLE INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT. The demurrer to the negligent misrepresentation cause of action is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. Studio Homes #1, L.P. has leave to plead the claim with more particularity. It has not alleged the time and nature (oral or written) and specific content of the alleged misstatements, the questions that allegedly prompted those statements, and which individuals asked the questions and made the statements. See Cadlo v. Owens-Illlinois, Inc. (2004) 125 Cal.App.4th 513, 519 ("Each element in a cause of action for fraud or negligent misrepresentation must be factually and specifically alleged.") Studio Homes #1, L.P should also allege, if it can do so, that cross-defendant intended to induce reliance and lacked a reasonable basis to believe its statements. The court rejects cross-defendant's remaining arguments. Whether cross-defendant's conduct caused damages and whether cross-complainant justifiably relied on cross-defendant's statements are factual issues not amenable to resolution on demurrer in light of the allegations, which include an alleged right to rescission arising from the alleged misconduct and costs in seeking that remedy. The escrow instructions are not a shield against cross-defendant's alleged tortious conduct given the allegations that cross-defendant affirmatively responded inaccurately to questions. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN GIDDING VS. GLENDONBROOK WINES PTY LTD, A PRESUMED AUSTRALIAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, July 11, 2014, Line 13, DEFENDANT AMERICAN GUARANTEE AND LIABILITY INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The demurrer is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. Plaintiff has leave to plead specific allegations regarding how defendant's alleged misrepresentations caused him damage. There is no apparent causal nexus between defendant's alleged misrepresentations and the pre-existing Glendonbrook judgment. The judgment was entered against plaintiff prior to the allegedly fraudulent settlement discussions in the malpractice action. Plaintiff alleges that the alleged misrepresentations caused plaintiff to lose the malpractice settlement proceeds. Plaintiff, however, rescinded the settlement and also concedes that the court ordered the settlement enforceable. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: ARTHUR ZANELLO et al VS. MICHAEL MULLIGAN et al*****CASE TRANSFERRED TO LOS ANGELES COUNTY*****
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Change Of Venue
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, July 11, 2014, Line 14, DEFENDANTS MICHAEL MULLIGAN, GINA MULLIGAN, MICHAEL J. MULLIGAN DEVELOPMENT, INC.'S Motion For Change Of Venue. The motion to change venue is granted and the case is transferred to Los Angeles County. Venue for the accounting, breach of fiduciary duty, and negligence causes of action is in Los Angeles, defendants' place of residence. See CCP 395. Moreover, the action, at least in part, concerns partnership obligations that arose prior to the written contract. The contract claim is also properly venued in Los Angeles since that is where Defendant entered into the contract and was to perform it, but, even if the breach of contract cause of action were properly venued in San Francisco, the joinder of the other claims compels granting this motion. "In a mixed action, a plaintiff alleges two or more causes of action each of which is governed by a different venue statute¿In cases with mixed causes of action, a motion for change of venue must be granted on the entire complaint if the defendant is entitled to a change of venue on any one cause of action." Brown v. Sup. Crt. (1984) 37 Cal.3d 477, 488. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: CITI-NETWORKING GROUP, INC VS. DERRICK A RAMIREZ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction (Ex Parte Papers Are Deemed Moving Papers; Opposition Due 7/07; Reply Due 7/09)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, July 11, 2014, Line 16, Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction. Continued to July 22, 2014. The July 1, 2014 Order granting the Temporary Restraining Order is ambiguous as to whether the Order to Show Cause pertains solely to preserving evidence, or also applies to the other relief sought for access and passwords. At the July 22, 2014 hearing, the Court will consider whether a preliminary injunction should be issued for all the relief sought in Plaintiff's Ex Parte Application for a Temporary Restraining Order. Defendant may file a sur-reply, not exceeding 15 pages, by July 17, 2014 that responds to Plaintiff's reply brief and evidence. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/PJB)
